proposed_title: How to Prove Customer Authorization in a Shopify Chargeback
summary: Focus on what actually counts as persuasive authorization evidence in card-not-present commerce. Cover account continuity, device clues, prior orders, and consent signals.

proposed_title: How to Review Shopify Checkout for Chargeback Risk
summary: Turn checkout review into a dispute-prevention exercise. Cover subscription disclosures, shipping promises, refund terms, post-purchase messaging, and confusing UX patterns.

proposed_title: How to Reduce Chargebacks Caused by Subscription Cancellation Friction
summary: Show how hidden, slow, or broken cancellation flows trigger avoidable billing disputes. Cover UX, customer communication, and what merchants should preserve as proof.

proposed_title: How to Reduce Chargebacks From Confusing Billing Descriptors
summary: Explain how descriptor clarity affects both fraud-coded and service-coded disputes. Cover brand mismatch, support visibility, and how to make charges easier for customers to recognize.

proposed_title: How to Standardize Chargeback Handling Across Multiple Shopify Stores
summary: Help merchant groups and aggregators create one operating model across several stores. Cover shared taxonomies, permissions, dashboards, and exception handling.

proposed_title: How to Structure a Chargeback Evidence Library
summary: Show merchants how to store, name, and version reusable evidence assets so deadline-driven cases are easier to build. Differentiate raw records from final exhibits.

proposed_title: How to Write a Chargeback Narrative That Actually Helps the Reviewer
summary: Explain the structure of a persuasive merchant narrative. Cover chronology, claim rebuttal, evidence references, and why concise factual writing works better than emotional argument.

proposed_title: Immutable Audit Logs for Dispute Operations: Why They Matter
summary: Explain the value of immutable history in dispute operations. Focus on accountability, QA, training, and proving who changed or submitted what when cases get messy.

proposed_title: Negative Option Billing on Shopify: What Merchants Must Get Right
summary: A practical guide for continuity sellers on disclosures, reminders, cancellation, and evidence. The aim is fewer billing disputes and better defendability.

proposed_title: Post-Purchase Emails That Reduce Chargebacks on Shopify
summary: Show which post-purchase messages reduce confusion and disputes after checkout. Cover order confirmation, shipping updates, delivery confirmation, subscription reminders, and support visibility.

proposed_title: Preorders and Backorders: Preventing Chargebacks When Fulfillment Slips
summary: Show how preorder and backorder merchants can reduce disputes during delays. Cover expectation-setting, updates, refund options, and what to document when timelines change.

proposed_title: Refund Denial Email Template That Lowers Escalation Risk
summary: Offer a customer-facing email template for denying a refund without unnecessarily provoking a bank dispute. Focus on clarity, empathy, and next steps.

proposed_title: Shopify Chargeback Fee Explained: When You Get It Back and When You Do Not
summary: Answer the fee questions merchants actually ask: what the fee is, when it is charged, whether it comes back, and how handling differs by provider context.

proposed_title: Shopify Chargeback Response Workflow for Small Teams
summary: Map a lean but effective workflow for merchants without dedicated risk staff. Focus on speed, role clarity, reusable evidence patterns, and where software removes manual drag.

proposed_title: Shopify Fraud Analysis and Chargebacks: How to Use Risk Signals Better
summary: Show merchants how to treat Shopify risk indicators as operational inputs rather than post-loss excuses. Cover holds, review rules, and proof preservation.
